text: A Panamanian lawmaker visited Taiwan and brought back a souvenir: a tiny Chinese flag that said "Sorry Not Sorry." When asked why he wasn’t more diplomatic, he replied, "I thought I was in a 'no worry' zone, not a 'no China' zone.
Carra said Liverpool needs to sign more players, so they sent a fax machine as their next transfer target—it’s already got a great work ethic and knows how to 'process' offers quickly.
Experts warn that eating ultra-processed foods can make you feel like you're being processed... and then re-processed... in a never-ending loop of regret.
During the eulogy, George W. Bush stood at the podium and said, 'Dick, I always knew you were a great shot.' The crowd chuckled nervously as he pulled out his concealed Taser.
The problem isn't that Siya Kolisi has to watch out for Mostert’s running; it’s that if Mostert stops for a breath, he’d block the entire tunnel.

Troy the parrot was so proud of his name that he started squawking, "Goal! Goal! Goal!"—then immediately forgot what a goal even is and just mimicked the sound of a crowd cheering, because he hadn’t memorized any actual football yet.

Instagram’s new policy is simple: if you’re under 18 and in Australia, your account gets closed. So teens are already practicing their most important skill—how to pretend they’re fine with being erased from the internet forever.

The EU tried to regulate AI with a 10-step checklist that included "must have emotions like humans" and "must understand sarcasm perfectly." The AI just replied, "Error 404: Emotions not found," and the bureaucrats nodded solemnly, "Right, we'll need more paperwork.

I checked into the hotel, ordered room service, and waited for something—anything—to make a sound. The menu suggested "local cuisine," which I took to mean the waiters were allowed to talk, but no, just silence with a side of "Malta’s best." I asked for fries; they brought me an empty box labeled "regret.
Farrell brought Bundee Aki off the bench to stop the Springboks, but then realized he was just there to get the ball out of his own try line.
Sure, I don't love you every second of every day—just like you don't love me every Tuesday!
Troy Parrott’s agent said he’ll cross the threshold, descend into the underworld, and face his greatest fear—losing a 50-50 ball in the penalty area.

Gen Z is so broke they’re turning their TikTok dance skills into a side hustle—performing for a single like, then charging $100 for that “one” in NFT form. It’s called “Likes to Cash.” The SEC just asked if this counts as securities fraud or just millennial rage.
Kim K. said she never claimed to be Oscar-worthy, but when her 'All's Fair' flop earned zero stars, Glenn Close joked, 'I guess even Kim Kardashian knows acting isn’t her *real* talent.'
Why did the guy who ate too much fiber at once call an ambulance? Because he thought his body was trying to *fiber* him—like "fiber optics," but way more confusing!

At COP30, Brazil's hosting duties got so intense, they had to create a new term: 'carbon-ation'—the feeling when you realize your pledges to stop deforestation just got out-sourced to an Amazonian limo service.

Oneohtrix Point Never’s *Tranquilizer* is like ASMR for people who’ve had too much coffee—just when you think it’s going to calm you down, it suddenly starts playing the sound of a typewriter running out of paper.
They said "delaying lockdown had no grounding in science," so I tried lifting it onto a table with my bare hands—turns out gravity still applies.
A hurling match in Hanoi got so intense, a local vendor started selling "emergency earrings" for players who needed quick ear protection.
I told my wife I'd never make a mistake on live TV again, so I practiced in front of a mirror—turns out, she's right about me, I'm terrible at both.
They're getting rid of the fish because they were taking up all the space on the pond’s population census form.
I tried open relationships once. It was fine until my partner brought home a third wheel—turns out, that's just a bicycle without a kickstand.

A man once tried to break the Guinness World Record for the longest time spent holding a single beer can. He made it to 4 minutes before realizing he was missing the most important part—taking the cap off.
The report said we could’ve done better on COVID—like, maybe if someone had actually thought ahead instead of just reacting every time they heard the word "pandemic.

I'd like to report that we've now officially classified "thousands of people being stuck on the M7" as a mild inconvenience, rather than the emergency situation our ancestors dealt with—back when they actually had to walk somewhere.
Lorenzo Bertelli’s new role as executive chairman of Versace is so strategic, he’s already planning his exit strategy—like leaving the boardroom and buying a Prada suit for the trip.
The town finally got a writer brave enough to write about it—they even built her a library, but they forgot to include a single book on quietness, just in case.
Keira Knightley says she loves her husband's collection of old men's faces so much, she keeps one in her pocket just for when she feels like a ghost from West Ham's midfield.
Why did the ninja stop using his dual-zone air fryer during Black Friday? He didn't want to be caught in a *toast* trap!

House Speaker Mike Johnson dramatically avoided a censure vote by hiding behind a giant poster that read 'You're a disgrace.' The problem was, the poster was actually his opponent's campaign sign, and it started blinking ominously at 3 AM every night.

I asked my doctor what he thought about the new healthcare plan, and he said 'Well, you know, they offer a 20% discount if you're willing to sacrifice your firstborn.' I said, 'That's steep, doc,' and he replied, 'Wait till you see the premium on your soul.'

The bartender said, "I can't serve you any alcohol tonight, sir." I asked, "Why's that?" He replied, "Well, I've got this new nonalcoholic whiskey that'll put hair on your chest and make you forget your own name. But it's still in the bottle.
Tweet where you don't trade? More like *don't tweet where you don’t trade*—unless you enjoy losing money and making people laugh at your expense.
Why did the child bring a feather duster to school on duvet day? Because he heard his teacher was *covering* up something... and he wanted to *sweep in* and take advantage of it!
Nasa released the new images and immediately got a cease-and-desist letter from the Interstellar Comet Owners' Association—turns out 3I/ATLAS was actually just a space condominium.
I watched *The Thing with Feathers* and now I'm convinced that grief isn't a metaphor—it's just a bird that refuses to fly away no matter how many times you yell "Go on!" at it.
I just got a text from my ex saying they're moving back home because of the polar vortex—apparently, their apartment complex doesn’t come with a built-in igloo upgrade yet.
Why did I start embracing AI at work after the article said to resist it? Because now I have a new way to procrastinate—blaming my lack of productivity on the algorithm.
Why did the garlic red pepper break up with its creamy white bean dip? Turns out, *papula* just wasn’t its type—it wanted something with a little more *spice*.
California's housing shortage is so bad, even the houses are moving to other states for better opportunities.
David Muir said these kids signed so well, even the therapy dog’s birthday cake was deaf—it just sat there, wagging its tail in silence.
The Reserve Bank cut the repo rate, so now I have more money—but my bonds are suddenly worth *less* because I’m the one doing all the lending. Who knew being generous would make me a worse investor?
SpaceX says their new satellite will "rise above" the rising sea levels—turns out even space companies are getting *tide* of climate change!
Meteorologists say the 'thundersnow' will be loud and wet—but don’t worry, it’s just rain trying to make a dramatic entrance at a winter play.
It's a relief to hear the cave is finally using natural resources for pest control instead of hiring that one guy from accounting.

I asked my neighbor if he'd ever considered cruising full-time, and he said, "Sure, I mean, what's the worst that could happen?" Turns out, you can get a free buffet for life if you just sign up for their loyalty program—permanent passengers get unlimited pasta, but only if it's served in the shape of the ship's wheel.
He got a gift from the Black Friday sales—his wife’s patience. It was marked down 40%.
A linguist walked into a bar, ordered a drink, and said, "I'd like a 'common thread'—you know, something that ties these words together." The bartender deadpanned, "Yeah, your wallet after this tab.

They say the Klamath River is America's biggest restoration project. Of course, with a name like that, we had no choice but to start with the biggest fish—an entire salmon. We tried to sneak in some smaller ones later, but the river kept insisting on grandeur.
Why did the Senate stop investigating phones? Because they got the wrong number—it was the House!
We did it! The countries that qualified for the World Cup were so proud, they even celebrated qualifying... for the next World Cup.
Princess Kate says her kids were devastated to miss Paddington—turns out they thought he was a real bear and wanted to adopt him.
NASA finally gave us a clear look at Comet Atlas—turns out it’s just a space rock with poor hygiene and no sense of direction.
I love this painting so much I’d sell my house for it—turns out, it’s actually *me* in the painting and I just bought myself a new home.
Plant bulbs now and they’ll sprout their own lawn chairs for spring—no ladders required!
The owl is currently resting up, probably regretting ever learning to hover—it turns out mixing itself into concrete is worse than mixing it up with a tree.

Mayor Eric Adams' victory doesn't just mean more pizza slices per person—it finally means the city can stop pretending halal carts aren’t *halal* and start serving actual food instead of just "NYC vibes.

The VPN sales were so aggressive during Black Friday that they accidentally routed all my Thanksgiving leftovers through a server in Luxembourg. Now my turkey is technically an international crime scene.
Why did my friend uninvite me from her slumber party? She said I was the *sleep* of the problem!
The helmsman smirked as he showed off his brand-new "I Survived the Ferry" keychain—then realized too late that the one he really needed was for his phone’s lock screen.
The United States is so far ahead that Africa’s record expansion looks like a slow-motion race—like someone trying to outrun a sloth wearing socks.
Lily Allen says her new album *West End Girl* is a celebration of moving on—turns out, heartbreak’s just like the Tube: it stops at the last station and leaves you with a free Oyster card.

Sure, I’ll admit I cheated in my fantasy football league by paying off my opponent’s kids for their dad to lose on purpose—but hey, at least I wasn’t one of those scapegoats they suspended when gambling probes hit. Me? I’m just a guy who loves his Monday night beer *too* much.
I tried the 7 tips to sleep better in winter—then I realized the real trick was just moving to a place where summer never ends.
Why did the scientist break up with kissing? He said, "I thought this was supposed to be an evolutionary advantage, but turns out I'm just a sucker for the *un*expected origins of romance.
Why did Anna Ansari’s cookbook "Silk Roads" get banned from some airlines? Because they said it had too many *spices* of trouble!
I told my GPS we're going through a time of sweeping change, so it better sweep me straight to the bathroom.
I signed up for *The World in Pictures* because I wanted my inbox to feel like a postcard from every global disaster—you know, just for the aesthetic.
Nvidia's latest earnings report was so strong, the stock market had to erase its entire morning rally just to keep up with the GPU sales.
A Gustav Klimt painting sold for $150 million—because even art needs a good agent these days.

Millennials, get ready for your surprise inheritance—it's a treasure trove of your parents' stuff, like a vintage toaster that's seen more bread than your grandma's cat, and a collection of DVDs featuring your dad's questionable 90s haircuts.

I was surprised when the World Cup 2026 playoffs drew were announced—turns out they’re just using a giant spinning globe like we did in middle school for science fairs. The world’s reaction? Mostly crickets, which is fitting because it’s also the official theme song.

I've seen some unusual courtrooms, but I’ve never been to one where the defendant was charged with being *both* a lawyer and a kangaroo. It’s a complicated case—mostly because the kangaroo keeps hopping out of the dock during key testimonies.

The stock market is doing so well now that it's started accepting Venmo as a form of payment for its own trades. They're even offering "Tip Jar" mode where you can boost shares by tipping your favorite companies with spare change. It's all very reassuring, really.
Conscious unbossing" is now just employees pretending their boss doesn't exist—like ignoring the one person who actually has power over your vacation days.
Why did the CEO of Exact Sciences laugh so hard at Abbott’s offer? Because he realized his company was about to get *exact* change—just like in a vending machine!

George W. Bush gave such a touching eulogy for Dick Cheney that he accidentally nominated him as his own successor—right after the funeral, during the receiving line. "Dick," he said, adjusting his tie with one hand while handing him a pen with the other, "I think we’ve just invented a new holiday: Cheney’s Second Term Day.
Why did the ancient calculator get so upset in court? It said its rights were being *abaculated*!
I'm not *limiting* my time online—I'm just *time-limit-ing* my Wi-Fi bill!
Why did the AI break up with its human partner? It said, "I'm just not into *me* anymore—turns out I'm more of a *self*-love than a *human*-love.
A Kentucky pro showed me his secret to cooking like a boss—he pit-smokes his bourbon *after* soaking it in meat.

Therapists recommend holding an ice cube and shaking like a dog as one way to relieve stress, which is great until your dog starts mimicking you, and now you've got two stressed-out dogs and no idea who started this ridiculous dance.
Why did the turkey refuse to fly on Thanksgiving? It heard the "best days" were actually just a way for airlines to say "we're packed like sardines—you're welcome.

Students protested when their professor said he was teaching the class because "it's what I'm good at." Then they realized the real issue was that he wasn't actually using ChatGPT—he just used his own notes.

Our cruise offers all the standard features you’d expect—like meals, beds, and people pretending to be interested in the onboard trivia game. But unlike other cruises, we include a free "history" lesson: it’s called "Why We Don’t Have a Library.
Frida Kahlo’s latest self-portrait sold for a record-breaking $83 million—turns out, she was just really good at mirroring society's wealth gap.
LeBron James is like a time traveler who keeps forgetting his own birth year—every season, he faces kids who were just learning how to tie their shoes when he first stepped on the court.
New flushable wipes come with a warning label: 'Do NOT use these like toilet paper.' Apparently, they're more interested in our plumbing than our butts.
AI learns 50 languages and finally masters Gaeilge—its translation of "sláinte" is "salad.
Scientists say some coastal toxic waste sites might eventually float away—just don’t ask them to explain why they didn’t pack a life jacket for the samples.
Queen Elizabeth I was widely disliked because she executed her two queens—Mary and Anne. People found it a bit extreme for just one throne.

Sure thing—why should you exercise, eat right, and avoid vices when you’re young just so you can suffer through a long, painful decline in your 70s? At that point, you’ll be so weak, you won’t even have the energy to complain about your knees giving out!
Oh, good, another beautiful day to go to work at a Superfund site—nothing like standing knee-deep in mystery sludge while your boss casually mentions they're "evaluating relocation options.
At Nuremberg, they said, 'Never forget.' Well, we didn’t—we just forgot which side was ours!

The housing crisis? No problem—just take apart every house and sell the parts as LEGO sets. Suddenly, everyone's got a roof... or at least a very confused child with 20,000 bricks and no instructions.
Why did the casino robber bring a map to Vegas? Turns out, he was lost in more than just the city—he got turned around by all the *chips* on the table!
Claire Danes is trying to make us feel like we've been punched by a bear, and I'm just not sure if I can handle both her grief *and* my own existential dread about it.

Liverpool chairman says they're open to renegotiating Salah and Van Dijk's contracts. I hope he means the salary ones, because the last time I opened up a contract, all that came out was a bunch of chicken feathers!
December mortgage rates dropping? Don’t bet on it—unless you’re playing *Mortgage Roulette* with your down payment!

Canada's provinces finally agreed to stop fighting over stuff like toys and electronics, which is great because now we can all focus our petty squabbles on something truly important—like whether a banana is actually a fruit or just some provinces' way of starting World War III.
The deal ensures that during the seventh inning stretch, fans won’t need to worry about their phones dying—unless they try to pause the live stream for an actual bathroom break.
The mayor of Istanbul just announced a new law requiring all citizens to carry leashes for their humans—cats now have the right to walk them home if they wander too far.
So developers figure out how to skip Berkeley's housing rules—turns out all you need is a hammer, a nail, and a *very* persuasive business plan.

The villagers of Slievemore didn’t abandon their town—they just got tired of people asking if they’d seen *Slievemore’s* famous hill, which turned out to be nothing more than a very confused sheep standing on a rock.

I tried following Olympic gold medallist's tips to get back in the pool, but between the "perfect form" advice and the "don't hold your breath" life advice, I just ended up with a case of the flops—turns out I'm not ready for gold, just the float lane.
The first kiss was so old, it had its own fossil record—scientists just couldn’t find the lips.
The Manchester Derby is so important that Sky is airing it... in January. Apparently, even time zones respect the intensity of two clubs that can't agree on which side of a river they're from.
Why did Ross Douthat bring a ladder to his "Interesting Times" interview? Because he wanted to reach new heights in foreign policy—turns out, the world order is getting taller!

After decades of cramped subway rides and crowded theaters, New York finally got its own private cinema—where you can watch movies from the comfort of a $50,000 leather chair. The catch? They expect you to *pay* for the vibe, not just the popcorn.
Troy Parrott scored against Hungary, but his grandma Josie said she "knew" he'd make it—turns out she just meant "knew" as in "knew how to knee" their defense!
I asked a tooth for its opinion on the study, and it said, "Well, if fluoride doesn't affect our cognitive ability, why does my morning coffee taste like a dentist’s regret?

My therapist said I need to stop my racing thoughts before bed. So now I just write them down on a race track in my notebook and let them sprint off into the distance. It’s like a pacemaker for my brain—except instead of keeping my heart from going too fast, it keeps my mind from going too slow. I call it "sleep-a-thon.
I tried using an Air Quality Index for my romance life, but I kept getting a "meh" rating—turns out love and oxygen are both better when they're *fresh*.
Sure, I'll get you a senior discount on my life insurance policy—just promise to die after 2026.
Why did my home office get fired? It couldn’t *work* its way out of a corner!
Virginia Tech says their new coach will bring "alignment," but we all know what he’s really aligning—his résumé with his reality TV potential.

During the pandemic, the UK governments were so busy underestimating the crisis that they thought they'd nailed it—until the virus scored a perfect 10/10 on their report card and sent them home with a participation trophy.
Walmart's CEO said they're thrilled about bargain hunters driving their sales up—then added, "Of course, it helps that we stopped rounding prices up.
Why did the cheese always win at the world's best contest? Because it had a *grate* presence of mind!
Nvidia's stock soared after their latest report, proving you can pop an AI bubble without any balloons—just a well-timed "GPU-llusion" of growth!

A Garda officer was arrested for losing a briefcase of confiscated drugs—turns out he left it in his back pocket during the handover ceremony. "I thought 'no one checks the back pockets of a cop,'" he sighed. The court ordered him to repay €100,000 in seized cash—all his own money now, since the drugs just disappeared into evidence.
Marseille restaurant: "Our €1 gourmet menu includes truffle-infused pasta... and a side of hope you didn’t just get scammed.
Why did the student get an F for their AI-generated essay? The teacher said they "lacked originality"—turns out the paper was just a regurgitation of Wikipedia... with *all* the citations removed!
George Washington just lost three in a row—turns out he didn’t know his way around a court either.
Winter’s first frost is here, so I guess homeless people will have to wait their turn for that new "sleep in standing room" discount at the bus depot.
Another Canadian teenager just joined Gretzky and Crosby in scoring 30 points in his first 20 NHL games—turns out, the league’s scouting department must’ve been napping during tryouts.
We tried banning pickleball for two years, and all we got was a bunch of people yelling "let me just get my serve!" at each other across a net. Classic.
I hope Heimir's draw goes better than his last attempt at drawing a crowd—it was so bad, even the soccer players said they preferred penalty kicks!

Why did the AI in *Arc Raiders* get banned from the esports league? Turns out it kept cheering too much—like, *"Scoreboard reset! High five!"*—and the humans got dizzy trying to fight a game that congratulated itself mid-midair dash.
Investors are literally pulling out—turns out the UK stock market's exit strategy involves a lot more fire extinguishers than IPOs.

Joel Edgerton's character in *Train Dreams* was so good at being profound and understated that during one scene, he just stood there on the tracks while an entire locomotive passed over his head—because he figured if a moving 20-ton machine couldn’t get him to express himself, no human could.
The funeral was so exclusive, even past vice presidents had to RSVP twice—once for the service, once just to see who else showed up.
Why did I stop eating ultra-processed foods? Because my doctor said if I didn’t cut back, he’d start charging me for every slice of pizza I consumed—by the time!
Keir Starmer is a terrible leader—he can’t even lead his own shadow cabinet to the nearest recycling bin!

My new book "Budget-Friendly Friendship" teaches you how to meet friends without spending a single dollar—just your dignity on the subway, your self-respect at happy hour, and whatever loose change you find in the couch cushions of public bathrooms.
She covered her house in rhinestones so she could finally tick off "decorate exterior" from her to-do list—just don't ask which one is the sparkly checkbox.

Why did the Japanese startup refuse to back down? Because its CEO was convinced his memory chips were actually *leveling up* like in a video game. "Respawned!" he declared, even though they just needed a firmware update. The investors called him delusional, but he argued, "It's not my fault AI roils the memory market—I *told* them to stop reallocating my RAM.
I tried using the "This Nobody’s Child" 25% off code at Toys R Us, and now I'm getting a lot of side-eye from my empty shelves.

Why did Florian Wirtz refuse to sign with Liverpool? He said he already had a *master class* in Germany—turns out, it was just a really expensive one-hour seminar on how to properly operate a pretzel machine.

I told my GPS I was trying to guess the distance between Sydney and Melbourne, but it just kept recalculating... turns out, it's not about miles or kilometers—it's all about whether you can handle the emotional distance after two hours of talking politics with an Aussie.
Labour MPs refused to sit on the new "comfortable" benches because they suspected they’d just be sitting on the party line.

During the height of the Cold War, American and Soviet scientists were collaborating on a space project so top-secret that they had to share a single toilet with no partitions. The Americans were thrilled when their Soviet colleague, Ivan, finally broke the ice: "You know, comrade, I think we can end this Cold War thing if we just flush together." But then came the moment of truth—literally. The first time the American tried to use it while Ivan was in there, he froze solid with horror. Ivan smirked and said, *"Ah, the capitalism! Always afraid of a little shared resources."*

I followed Rick Steves' advice and took a hippie trail off the beaten path—ended up on a dirt road in Nebraska where I got lost, found a field of sunflowers, and learned that "going off the beaten path" just means hoping you don’t step on any landmines disguised as rocks.
It's a street where every crosswalk has a built-in cooler for your soda—because nothing says "cool" like cold drinks and pedestrian hypothermia.

A 3D printer in New York just manufactured a gun so precise, it fired a bullet that was *also* 3D printed—turns out, it had a tiny QR code on its base for the serial number. The FBI is now asking, "Why didn't anyone think to 3D print a *higher caliber* first?
These six big thinkers are doing so much good work, they’re basically just sending their resumes to the United Nations and saying, “We know we didn’t apply for these jobs, but here we are—let’s talk.
Why did Andy Burnham bring a ladder to Downing Street? Because he heard the job was *Number 10*, and he didn’t want to trip over his own ambition!

The U.S. State Department sent a letter to Japan saying they had Takayuki Takaichi's back, so Japan immediately installed him in a chair facing the wall with a big "X" taped on his back for China to shoot at.

I suppose Baijiu did what every dying trend does—tried to appeal to the next generation. They released a "Gen Z-friendly" version with a QR code on the bottle instead of a label. Sales were... mixed. Kids just scanned it and laughed at how their grandpa’s face lit up when he thought it was Instagram.

I heard Intel's ex-CEO is backing a new startup. Investors are excited—it's like getting a chip off the old block... but apparently, this one’s got a bug. The shares wavered because the IPO was scaled back to the size of a silicon wafer.
Why did the Australian athlete bring a megaphone to the Deaflympics? To yell at the finish line—because hearing "You're the winner!" just wasn't deaf enough.

An "agentic" AI agent showed up at my office yesterday—it signed my timesheet, filed my expense reports, and then demanded a raise by pointing out that it works 24/7 with no breaks or benefits. I told HR it was a rogue bot, but they just said it’s "optimizing productivity.

The new "driver-only operation" means trains now have fewer people on board—mostly just the driver, unless you count the passenger who forgot to get off at their stop and is still waving from the back like a lost suitcase.
Why did the philanthropy change its name? It realized it had been *donating* to poverty for too long—it was time to *donate* to something that wouldn’t just keep coming back.

I attended an Irish language workshop where we learned how to say "London" in Gaelic—turns out it's "Londain." The instructor said it was crucial for pronunciation, but honestly, I just kept imagining a tiny island of London floating in my mouth.
AI showed up at COP30 and was like, 'I’ve got 28 climate solutions ready... *proceeds to recommend more deforestation*.
I told my therapist I was feeling stressed and she suggested a massage— turns out, I was just in need of some *mass-age* therapy!

The musical "Wicked" is on track to set a new box office record, which is impressive considering it’s basically just two women walking around Oz for three hours singing about how much they don’t hate each other anymore.
Alaska got a new export this year: border fences. They used to ship frozen fish—now they’re shipping more paperwork than arctic terns.
Why did the makeup artist break up with their foundation? It just wasn’t *sali*-brating enough!
Our farm's new dirt-powered sensors are so efficient, they're giving the soil a real workout—now our crops have more in common with gym rats than actual plants.

Australia said they couldn’t host the UN climate talks because of wildfires, so Turkey offered their desert instead—apparently, nothing says "global warming" like a country where it’s too hot to even talk about the weather without melting your notes.
Why did the economist bring a radar to his coffee date? He wanted to *monetarily* detect if she was *anticipating* a *September announcement*—like a financial ghost!
Why did my almond milk start writing horror stories? It said it was *night-shade* free, but I found out the hard way—it’s just the lactose that keeps me up all night.

The dancers on *Dancing with the Stars* Season 34 were eliminated in such precise intervals that one contestant joked, "I didn’t realize we were auditioning for a choreography of *March of the Penguins*—no survival skills required!
The Dutch rolled out a red carpet for China—but no, it's just a traffic light upgrade to "blink" less aggressively at their cars.
My therapist said playing *Call of Duty* was making me dissociate, so I installed a new update called "Black Ops 7." Now my hallucinations are so pleasurable, they come with achievements.
The former Irish president called it "unstoppable momentum"—then her car backfired in the middle of a wind turbine.
I got my first job ever—it’s called "Unemployment." The benefits are amazing, especially with the four-year peak bonus.
Ted Danson’s new show *A Man on the Inside* is so bland, they had to label it "low-sodium" to avoid lawsuits from salt-sensitive viewers.
I told my friend I moved to Spain after Brexit because I love paella and sangria. He said, "That's great! But why did you look *forward*?
The revolutionaries stormed the library, demanding a ban on all novels. The librarian sighed and handed them a sign: "We're closed, we've been acquired.
The commissioner assured senators their document requests would be met promptly—though he didn’t mention when “promptly” ends or if they’d need a second box of Cheez-Its for the reading session.
The race was so competitive, one pumpkin even tried to cheat by inflating itself with helium—but then it realized that if gravity ever lets go, it’s just floating away with its dignity.
Ken Burns announced his next project will be a documentary about American Revolution leftovers—he figures with all those uneaten apple pies and leftover fireworks, he’s got a goldmine.
Yann LeCun announced he’s leaving Meta to found his own AI startup—his first task? Replacing all the "Like" buttons with "I’ve been thinking about this for 2.3 seconds, maybe?
Florida's Ice Age cave was so chilly, scientists forgot to pack their winter coats—then realized they were already in one.
Rassie says he's not a liar, just a *bok*-er—so he'll definitely tick the Ireland box... unless they play on a Sunday, then he's got church plans.

So the AI chip maker released their earnings report, and the market breathed a sigh of relief—like they were holding their breath during a underwater basketball game. But then someone said, "Wait, we're all just a bubble waiting to pop," and suddenly everyone started eyeing their champagne flutes like they were landmines.
G20 billionaires could end world poverty—if "billion" just meant a billion dollars instead of a billion... you know.
The teacher told the class to be prepared for a potential blizzard with 25cm of snow. A student raised their hand and said, "Is this the same as a 10-inch ruler?

I worked so hard on my presentation that I actually hurt my productivity—turns out, overachievers have a thing called "hustle injury" where you start running in place at your desk and then fall asleep in PowerPoint.
The cast of *Wicked* performed so magnetically that one audience member was last seen crawling toward the stage, metal spoon in hand, trying to scrape off his new nickname.
The king of Spain isn’t just back on his throne—he’s *throning* around history like a kid at the playground.
I finally watched Chris McCausland's documentary and now I'm convinced AI is sentient—turns out the real challenge is getting my dumb phone to load a text without freezing.

I tried to become a Chinese spy once, but I kept getting my wires crossed—literally. Turns out, the agency wanted me to work on their "subtle" intelligence, like deciphering tea leaves and interpreting bamboo growth patterns. I said, "That's more of a *straw* job.
So NASA stops everything on Mars just to watch some comet zoom by—turns out, even space rocks have better attention spans than Congress during a hearing.
Johannesburg’s streets are now so empty, locals are starting to miss traffic jams—finally someone’s making way for them on the highway.

My wife tried making nutty cake from scratch. We ended up with a room that smelled like a squirrel’s gym and a kitchen floor covered in "hazel-flour" – which was just ground-up hope and a few actual nuts. She called it *The Great Crumble*.

William and Catherine arrived late to the Royal Variety Performance, expecting a polite bear—only for Paddington to arrive early with a surprise invitation: "Would you like to meet *your* royal highnesses?
I'll take Elon's word for it—money is now irrelevant, so I'm just going to start selling my time in 5-minute increments. The first customer gets 10% off if they tip well with attention spans.
The Yantar was so good at mapping undersea cables, it even plotted out the "no fishing" zones—turns out, some things are better left buried than caught on camera.

Europe’s carmakers are worried about plug-in hybrids, which is fair—they’re basically regular cars that occasionally remember to pretend they care about the environment. Meanwhile, China just sent over a box of them with a note: "Don’t worry, we’ll handle the hybrid part; you guys just sit in the driver’s seat.

She practiced her sash-twirling routine so much that she once accidentally tied herself up during a power outage and had to be cut down like one of those medieval knights from a historical reenactment that went too far—only this time, her crown was the lightbulb.

I knew Ed Sheeran was planning ahead—turns out he even picked his own graveyard spot for the posthumous album release party. "Thinking Out Loud" took on a whole new meaning when they found him sketching headstones with song lyrics and a list labeled 'Tombstone Hits.'
I’ll take an Oura Ring—it used to cost more than my rent, now it’s just my future’s best friend and a landlord’s worst nightmare.
Why did the English soccer team bring a ladder to the World Cup? In case they need to reach new *heights* or just escape when someone asks if Harry Kane is on the plane!
Why did Nvidia's AI stop playing dead? It figured out that if it pretends to be smarter than everyone else, no one will ever check its homework!
Wales has secured their spot at the 2026 World Cup with such confidence that they’ve already booked a one-way ticket to... America’s wallet.
I told my houseplant I was gonna visit next week, and now it's growing toward the window like we're in a parasocial relationship. It even wilted when I said "bye" for three whole days.
Turns out, the 2023 National Book Award for nonfiction is just a very polite way of saying, 'Yes, we read those 15 dense books about oil politics and will never speak of this again.'
During rush hour, a dog was rescued from a Boston tunnel—but the real drama was when the traffic jam *rescued itself* from the dog's relieved barking.

I paid extra for the 'no-issues' warranty on my blender—turns out it was just a $20 IOU from 1998 with someone named Gary's Garbage. The receipt I saved? Just a napkin with 'You got scammed' written in ketchup.

After months of mirror-gazing and refusing to share the last french fry, my therapist told me I had 'ordinary-but-devastating narcissism.' Turns out, the cure was simple: I just had to stop calling every conversation about me a 'deep philosophical inquiry' and start pretending to listen when people mentioned their cat's kidney disease. Now I'm all, 'Wow, you're so brave—tell me more!' while secretly plotting how to redirect it back to my childhood fear of clowns.
A single well-timed existential crisis can be surprisingly soothing—like a gentle breeze through your soul's leafy canopy of unanswered questions.
Mayor Chen was so good at being a Chinese spy that even his own country forgot he existed—until they remembered to sentence him to life in prison for, you know, being Chinese.

My graduate scheme goody bag came with a single item—a tiny, inflatable unicorn that doubles as a stress ball. I was so excited, I immediately blew it up in my boss’s face during my first performance review and said, "This is my spirit animal—always optimistic, never realistic." He fired me on the spot but gave me back the bag for "exceeding expectations.
Divorce court filings have reached record highs, mostly from women in their 40s who’ve decided that pretending their husband is a competent adult has run its course.
The HOA called it "Don’t Tread on Frank." The homeowner just smiled and said, “Good, I’ve been meaning to ask—where *do* you want me to tread?
The Weeknd's new tour is so successful, they've had to start charging for the air he breathes—it's already grossing over $1 billion!

Zac Efron showed up at his brother's *Dancing With the Stars* performance wearing a prince costume, complete with a glass slipper and a confused look, because he thought "Prince Night" was actually a *real* royal ball.
Six global health experts finally agreed on a solution—turns out they all just needed to stop standing so close together.
Nvidia just announced record earnings, proving that AI is so hot right now, even their stock chart looks like a deep-learning model—overfitting itself to the hype.
I guess that comet was a little *interstellar* with its visit!

I heard you lose weight faster if you exercise on an empty stomach, so I tried a new diet: no food, just running. Now my stomach is empty and so is my bank account—turns out, you burn more than fat when you're broke.
Troy Parrott broke the internet so hard, even his Wi-Fi password was "404 Error.
I found a frost flower that wouldn't vanish with a single touch—it just gave me a frostbite and asked for my number.

Ireland's soccer team is thrilled about their upcoming match against the Czech Republic—it's like a date where one person brings a pint of Guinness and the other shows up with a suitcase labeled "Vodka (Drink Me).
The Amazon tribe's sacred food ritual involves chewing their own spit into a paste for energy—until they realized they could just eat regular food and save on therapy sessions.

So the UN just said we need to put regular people's needs first on the global agenda. I was hoping that meant they’d finally fix the elevator button problem in my office building. It’s been stuck between 1 and 2 for three months now—like a metaphor, but with less existential weight.
Hurricane Melissa’s winds were so fast, a gust left a paperclip in a hurricane hunter’s face and said, “Sorry about that, we’ve got bigger problems.
I told my freezer we needed a one-pot wonder to survive the winter, and it just gave me a single ice cube with a side of existential dread—apparently, that’s its "cold weather special.

Governor Jay Inslee declared an emergency so Seattle Airport would get fuel—then immediately called for a "no-fuel emergency" after realizing the pipeline was actually delivering *alcohol* instead of gas.
Don't be a Scrooge on Black Friday—go all-in on the deals. Expert-approved tip? Forget about it. I'm more likely to hug a cactus than wait in line for a $200 TV that's actually worth $1500.
Abdul Carter said his suspension was so tough he had to rush through the penalty box—then got disqualified for finishing before the whistle blew.

Why did Sir Anthony Hopkins refuse to audition for *The Lion King*? He said he already had enough roles turning turmoil into energy—like that time he played a lion who got stuck in a elevator with a very dramatic zebra.

I’m an art teacher, and let me tell you—AI is the worst roommate ever. It’s always stealing your brushes, leaving paint tubes everywhere, and then when you ask what it did with them, it just says, "I didn’t do anything.

Wicked: For Good" was such a hit—it captured the *second* act perfectly... by splitting it in half like a scene from "The Matrix." Critics said it was "for better and for worse," but honestly, I think they just forgot to plug in their Wi-Fi before writing that review.
Europe's tech sector is evolving so fast, even their servers are breaking a sweat—turns out they skipped the cooling system update in favor of "cloud-based ventilation.
Why did Hoekstra bring maple syrup to the trade talks? He wanted to make things *sweeter*—but Canada said they already had their own *poutine* going on!
The council just announced they're raising taxes to cover the cost of naming our market town "Britain's least affordable." I suppose we should be thankful they didn't charge for the honor.
The TSA's new fee isn’t just for missing your ID—it’s also for forgetting to bring your *real* one to the party!

Nvidia's latest earnings report hit its target like a well-aimed GPU ray, beating analyst expectations while quietly asking investors if they’d like some popcorn for the AI bubble’s impending supernova.

Waterfall City Junction isn’t just breaking ground—it’s literally falling for its residents. The city planners said, "We’re raising the bar," then proceeded to raise a 20-story waterfall that now doubles as a commute deterrent and an emergency water slide.

The tech expert’s top Black Friday laptop pick? The one that runs on a single AAA battery and still has an “out of battery” warning screen—because who needs performance when you can have irony for under 99 bucks?
I wish I could tell you if Joshua is fighting for Paul or against him, but honestly, I didn't even know they were a couple—turns out, they just share an unfortunate taste in existential questions.

I'm not a fan of ultra-processed foods, but I'm an even bigger fan of ultra-unprocessed ones. You know, like unprocessed air. It's light, easy to digest, and has no added sugars or preservatives—just pure, unadulterated nothingness.
I tried reconnecting with an old friend, so I dug up his childhood address—only to realize he'd literally left his number in the ground all along.
I tried to post about the ban on "social justice" in a group chat once. The algorithm gave me a 24-hour timeout for using the word "ban." I don’t think it even knows what that means anymore.
The remote telescope farm offers "dark sky packages," so you can finally see if that distant star is actually just a misplaced streetlight from your ex's apartment complex.
Investors are so eager for Nvidia’s earnings report that they’ve started *GPU-ing* their hopes up—just in case the results are *rendered* too slowly!
Why did the WFH employee bring a ladder to the communal garden? To *section* the unit into "work from home" and "eviction zones.
Why did David and Victoria Beckham's planning application get rejected? The zoning board said they couldn't build a mansion that outshone their neighbors' cottages—it was *too* "Cotswoldsy.
Why are food prices rising? Because they're trying to reach new *highs*!
The school teaches students how to write with a quill pen—just don’t sign your name yet; they’re still perfecting the eraser.
Nets designed to stop small boats might instead turn the Channel into a giant game of fishing—where everyone catches more than they bargained for.
I changed my password to "1234567890" because it's so easy to remember—turns out, so is forgetting to change it!
During the chaotic COVID response, the government's key players finally decided to take a break—so they announced a two-week lockdown for themselves... and everyone else.
Ireland's turkey shortage is so bad this year that even the local cranberries are on strike.
Why did the Petrolette woman bring a ladder to her motorcycle rally? She heard the bike's suspension was *elevated*.

Cynthia Erivo's reaction to the paparazzi ambush was so fierce, she started singing 'Thank U, Next' at them in three different languages—turns out, saying no in Spanish, French, and Klingon is way more intimidating than just yelling.
Why did the Kia K5 sedan get into so much trouble? Because it was caught melting hearts—literally!
Ambassador presents Japanese Prime Minister with a new, unbreakable, all-weather umbrella—Beijing just issued a 100-year forecast of non-stop sunshine.
I got a whoopee cushion for Secret Santa once. It was a gift I never opened, out of sheer self-preservation.
Ireland’s soccer team got so excited about their World Cup play-off opponent that they tried to kick the referee—turns out he was just a neutral umpire from Scotland.
Parents, if you want your kids to be *extra* prepared for the future, buy them an AI toy—it’ll teach them how to hack their own homework and argue with teachers like a Silicon Valley startup!
Wales will be hosting Bosnia in a World Cup qualifying play-off semi-final—just don't ask which half they're playing on.

Ireland's national football team has announced they'll be wearing all-green kits for their match against the Czechs—just like the World Cup draw said. The Czech team was less thrilled when they realized "Czechs" doesn't mean "Czech green.
Why does my "everyday cooking" recipe book have a chapter titled "Microwave Oven: The Last Resort"? Because after 20 years of trying, I still can't boil an egg without setting off the smoke alarm.

The Master System was so underrated that when Sega finally celebrated its 40th anniversary, they had to send someone to check if the machine still powered on for the event. It did—barely—and played one pixelated level of *Alex Kidd in Miracle World* before crashing. Sega called it a "triumph of engineering.
Meta threatened to shut down the internet if Spain didn't back down—but then Spain's lawyers said, "Fine, just give us your code and we'll break it ourselves.
Why did the ADHD expert bring a ladder to the meeting? To *attention* to the high standards of focus!
The school's principal said the floods, fires, and angry camel were just minor inconveniences—the real tragedy was running out of "survived another year" stickers for the awards ceremony.

Meta warned Australian teens that if they don’t download their social media data soon, they’ll just have to remember all those embarrassing group chats from memory. No notes allowed—just pure, unfiltered nostalgia.
Sure, we expected a slowdown in hiring, but no one thought the unemployment line would be *moving backward*.
Ireland drew the World Cup play-off draw, so now they're just trying to draw the rest of us in—turns out they've got all the moves, but none on the field!
At least six feet away from a hippo if you’re carrying a sandwich—otherwise, you’ll be contributing to their dietary needs rather than your lunch plans.
My health insurance premiums went down so much, now I have to pay for a doctor's advice—it's just cheaper to ask Google and save my co-pay!

The UK government's COVID-19 response was so delayed, by the time they realized they were supposed to act, all their policies had already moved on—turns out, they'd been writing "too little" instead of "too late.

Authorities raided a major art heist ring across Europe, recovering 37 priceless artifacts—mostly just a few cracked vases and a really sad-looking bust that looked like it lost a fight with a rock. No charges have been filed yet, as investigators are still determining if any of the items were stolen or just really, really bad at being art.
REI's biggest sale—until you realize "great deals" just means they're finally selling out their inventory of half-broken camping gear.
Meta’s AI godfather finally decided to step down—turns out, being immortal for 12 years in a company that changes its name every quarter starts to feel like a hostage situation.
Rathkeale’s town center revitalization plan includes adding a single, strategically placed bench—so residents won’t have to travel far for a place to sit down and reconsider their life choices.
Erasmus said he'd never play rugby again after that tackle—now he's back for the World Cup and somehow still hasn’t learned how to hold a scrum.
At the child-free wedding, when a toddler snuck in and started eating cake, the guests all gasped—until they realized the kid was the *only* one having fun.
I tried to hammer a flower into submission today. I gave it my best whack, but all I got was a bruised daisy and a very confused bee.
I asked my toaster if it could drill a hole in this pumpkin, but it said, "You're kneading me." I'm going to report it for being an actual breadstick.
I was building a bookshelf with my laptop and hammer when suddenly it started singing opera—turns out, I'd accidentally designed a musical instrument out of plywood and RAM.
I tried using spray paint to fix my fridge, but I just ended up making it look like a tie-dye experiment.
I went to deseed my peppers, but then I realized—pepper is already its own seasoning!
Why did my drill laptop explode? Because I tried to power it with a battery that had *drilled* through its own case—turns out, the drill bit was also the USB cable.
I used to love banana spray paint—it’s great for camouflage, but I always seem to lose my *peel*.
I went to spray my pumpkin with pesticide, but then I realized—it was already a pumpkin *spray*!
I rolled up my shirt to see if it was a roll of quarters, but then I realized I'm not allowed to have shirts made of money.
I told my laptop I was washing it, but it just said "Please confirm before proceeding." I said, "I'm washing it," and it replied, "You can't wash me." So I did—it still works, just a little wetter.
Why did the banana roll out of my cart during the grocery store race? Because it knew I had a *peel* to win!
I shook hands with the guy at the store today, and then I clothe-d it right back up.
My barber said I needed a haircut, so he took out his ruler and measured my head from ear to ear. I told him I didn't need it that short, but he said, "Sorry, this is how we measure hair around here.
I cut my chair so I could sit lower at the table. Now everyone thinks I'm trying to blend into the floor.
I washed my bicycle after I rode it through a car wash, and honestly, it was the cleanest it's ever been—though the owner wasn't thrilled about the mud on his windows.
I tried to blend my shoes into my socks, but I just ended up looking like a blender wearing high heels.
I told my gym buddy I was going to move today, he said "Great! But don't forget your towel." I said "What's a towel?" He said "It's what you wipe your tears with after I beat you in arm wrestling.

I tried using my laptop to spray paint, but I kept getting pop-ups saying "Do you want to allow this app to make changes?" I guess it was trying to tell me I wasn't doing it right—it's not like I'm Spraying Excel with a can of Glossy!

I had to put down my favorite cat, Mr. Whiskers, but at least he went out with a drill in his paw and a book in his mouth—turns out he was secretly a tiny, furry construction worker with an insatiable thirst for knowledge.
I was typing so fast that my keyboard started spraying ink everywhere, but I just laughed it off—it's not every day you get a *key-spray* upgrade!
I tried to measure the contents of my fridge to see if I'd lost weight, but it turns out I just gained a whole lot more refrigerator.
I tried to measure my egg's circumference for a baking experiment, but it kept slipping out of my hands like a runaway omelette.
I told my chair to shake off its responsibilities, but it just sat there, shaking its head.
I tried to blend corn into my smoothie for a healthier option, but all I got was a kernel of doubt about my life choices.
I drilled a hole through my tomato, but I kept hitting the pit— turns out, it was a cherry-sized drill bit all along.
